aliases: Pancake breakfast set with sausage and hash brown
Pancakes with maple syrup, sausage patty and hashbrowns

english: Pancake breakfast set with sausage and hash brown
Pancakes with maple syrup, sausage patty and hashbrowns

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a pancake food product class (FOODON_00002473); the full set meal including sausage and hashbrown is not represented as a single composite entry. Closest parent match used for the pancake component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ covers pancakes as a cereal-based product. The composite set meal (pancake + sausage + hashbrown) does not have a single FoodEx2 code; pancake component used as primary anchor, representing a closest parent match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for this composite set meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal serving of approximately 300 g, comprising two pancakes with maple syrup and butter, one sausage patty, and one portion of hashbrowns.

nutrient_source.title: Pancakes with sausage and hashbrown — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Pancakes with sausage and hashbrown' (cr_id F08020041). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is not reported in the HPB record; estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogue (USDA SR Legacy: pancakes with sausage, NDB 21052) as approximately 6.5 g/100g. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite set meal. GI of 67 is estimated based on the pancake component as the dominant carbohydrate source. Pancakes (made from refined flour, milk, eggs) have a published GI of approximately 67 (medium) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The sausage and hashbrown components contribute negligible additional carbohydrate-driven GI effect but increase overall glycaemic load through fat-mediated gastric slowing. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 67 × (21.27 × 3.0) / 100 ≈ 42.8; rounded to 38 accounting for fat-slowing effect on gastric emptying. Serving size used: 300 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 270 mg/100g from processed sausage and seasoned hashbrown, combined with high saturated fat (~6.5 g/100g) promoting arterial stiffness, and low potassium (54 mg/100g) providing minimal vascular offset.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its high sodium content from processed sausage and hashbrown; if consumed, request grilled chicken sausage with reduced sodium, baked hashbrown, and avoid adding ketchup, maple syrup, or other high-sodium condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined-flour pancakes with a GI of ~67 (medium-high), combined with high fat (18.97 g/100g) and low dietary fibre (1.72 g/100g), produce a high glycaemic load per serving (~38) with no meaningful glycaemic modulation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this meal or consume it only occasionally in small portions; substituting wholemeal pancakes, baked hashbrown, and omitting maple syrup would substantially reduce the glycaemic load and improve postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (18.97 g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~6.5 g/100g from sausage and butter, combined with cholesterol (27.59 mg/100g), sodium (270 mg/100g), and likely trans fat from deep-fried hashbrown, creates a multi-pathway cardiovascular risk profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal due to its high saturated fat, sodium, and likely trans fat content; if consumed, request baked hashbrown, grilled lean sausage, and omit butter and maple syrup to reduce atherogenic load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Likely trans fat from deep-fried hashbrown, saturated fat and arachidonic acid from processed sausage, and advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) generated during deep-frying collectively promote systemic inflammation and joint damage.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal; the deep-fried hashbrown is a source of trans fat and AGEs, both of which are strongly pro-inflammatory, and the processed sausage contributes arachidonic acid and sodium nitrates that exacerbate joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pancakes with Sausage and Hashbrown is a Western-style breakfast set meal consisting of two or more soft, pan-fried pancakes made from refined wheat flour batter, served with maple syrup and butter, accompanied by a grilled or pan-fried pork sausage patty and deep-fried shredded potato hashbrowns. The pancakes are light and fluffy with a mildly sweet flavour enhanced by maple syrup; the sausage patty is savoury and seasoned with herbs and spices; the hashbrowns are crispy on the outside and soft inside. This meal is popular in fast-food and casual dining contexts in Singapore and internationally, often consumed as a breakfast or brunch item. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 303 kcal/100g, with high fat (18.97 g/100g, predominantly from sausage and deep-frying), moderate carbohydrates (21.27 g/100g from refined flour and syrup), and moderate protein (6.84 g/100g). Dietary fibre is low at 1.72 g/100g, and sodium is elevated at 270 mg/100g from processed sausage and seasoned potato. The glycaemic index is approximately 67 (medium), driven by refined-flour pancakes, yielding a high glycaemic load per standard serving (~38). Clinically, this meal is unsuitable for individuals with hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sodium, saturated fat, likely trans fat from deep-frying, and low fibre content. Healthier modifications include wholemeal pancakes, baked hashbrown, grilled lean sausage, and omission of maple syrup and butter.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08020041, confidence 0.95), which is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite analogue (pancakes with sausage) as the HPB record does not report this field. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogue for refined-flour pancakes. Overall confidence is high for macros and moderate for GI and saturated fat estimates.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this food item (F08020041), and no sufficiently close composite analogue in USDA FoodData Central provides reliable per-100g values for this exact set meal combination. 2. saturated_fat_g estimated at 6.5 g/100g from USDA SR Legacy entry for pancakes with sausage (NDB 21052) as the HPB record does not report saturated fat separately. 3. trans_fat_g is set to -1 (not reported by HPB); however, the health context notes acknowledge that deep-fried hashbrown likely contributes a small amount of trans fat (~0.1 g/100g estimated), which is clinically relevant for rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular disease assessments. 4. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on refined-flour pancakes from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite set meal. Glycaemic load per serving (~38) calculated using 300 g serving size. 5. This food is a Western-style set meal; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable as this is not a Singaporean-origin dish and no established local-language names exist for this specific product.
